questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: government / new / the / announced / policy / has / a / mining
options: The government has a new mining policy announced
Government has the new mining policy announced
The government has announced a new mining policy
The government has announced a new mining policy
Announced a new mining policy the government has
questions.solution: The government has announced a new mining policy

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Nyquist stability: encirclements of -1 point give:
questions.solution: N = P - Z. Encirclements of -1 (clockwise) = Z = P - N. Z must be 0 for stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The output of a linear time-invariant (LTI) system is the input convolved with the:
questions.solution: y(t) = x(t) ∗ h(t); the impulse response h(t) fully characterizes an LTI system.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The free-space wavelength of a 150 MHz signal is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which comparison of BJT and FET is correct?
options: BJT is voltage-controlled; FET is current-controlled
BJT is current-controlled; FET is voltage-controlled
questions.solution: BJTs are controlled by base current, FETs by gate voltage with very high input impedance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mass action law: n·p = n_i². For n=10¹⁶ cm⁻³ in Si:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ARM — exception vector table:
options: Reset, Undefined, SWI/SVC, Prefetch, Data, IRQ, FIQ. Vector addresses 0x00 or 0xFFFF0000
questions.solution: ARM vectors: 8 entries × 4 bytes. FIQ: fastest (banked registers). IRQ: standard interrupt.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Resonant converter zero current switching (ZCS):
options: Switch turns off when current crosses zero
questions.solution: ZCS: current reaches zero before turn-off. Reduces turn-off losses. Used in high-current applications.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Adaptive filter: in system identification, desired signal is:
questions.solution: System ID: adaptive filter models unknown system. Desired = actual system output; filter output should converge to match.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Microstrip effective dielectric constant:
questions.solution: εeff = (εr+1)/2 + (εr-1)/2 × correction. Field partly in air, partly in substrate → effective εr between 1 and εr.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Bit-banging: implementing protocol in:
questions.solution: Bit-banging: toggle GPIO pins in software to implement protocols (UART, SPI, etc.). Flexible but CPU-intensive.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Overshoot decreases as damping ratio ζ:
questions.solution: Higher ζ → less oscillation → lower overshoot. ζ=1: no overshoot.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 8085 instruction MOV A,M reads from:
options: Memory location pointed by HL pair
questions.solution: MOV A,M: A ← [HL]. HL holds memory address. One of indirect addressing modes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PID controller — derivative:
options: D = K_d·de/dt. Adds damping, improves stability. Amplifies noise
questions.solution: D: adds zero. Predictive. Filtering required. 'Rate' action.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 8085 addressing modes:
questions.solution: 8085: no indexed or relative addressing. 8086 adds indexed, based, based-indexed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Unity feedback system G(s)=K/(s(s+2)). Gain margin is infinite when:
questions.solution: G(jω)=K/(jω(jω+2)). Phase: -90°-arctan(ω/2). Max phase lag → -180° only as ω→∞. Gain→0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 8253/8254 timer has:
questions.solution: 8253: 3 counter channels. Each: 16-bit, 6 modes (interrupt on terminal count, rate generator, etc.).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PID controller — proportional:
options: P = K_p·e(t). Increases speed, reduces but does not eliminate SSE
questions.solution: P: simple. High K_p → oscillation. Steady-state error exists (type 0).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Non-inverting: Rf=22kΩ, R₁=22kΩ, Vin=3V. Vout:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Small-signal model of BJT at midband:
questions.solution: Hybrid-π: gm = I_C/V_T, rπ = β/gm, ro = V_A/I_C.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Second-order underdamped system: rise time t_r≈(π-cos⁻¹ζ)/(ω_n√(1-ζ²)). For ζ=0.5, ω_n=10:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The rectified output waveform shown (alternate gaps between pulses) is produced by a:
questions.solution: Only one half-cycle passes, leaving flat gaps — characteristic of half-wave rectification.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MOSFET gate is insulated by:
options: SiO₂ layer (very high input impedance, ~10¹⁴ Ω)
questions.solution: MOSFET: Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor. Enhancement or depletion mode. NMOS/PMOS/CMOS.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Gain margin: amount of gain increase before instability (at phase crossover):
questions.solution: GM = -20log|G(jω_pc)|. Positive dB: gain can increase before instability. Typical requirement: >6 dB margin.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Power amplifier — thermal considerations:
options: Heat sink required. Thermal resistance: θ_JA = θ_JC + θ_CS + θ_SA
questions.solution: Thermal: T_j = P_D·θ_JA + T_A. Max T_j: 150-200°C for Si. Derating above 25°C.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: BJT α-β relationship: α = β/(1+β). For β=99:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bode plot consists of:
options: Magnitude (dB) vs log ω and Phase (°) vs log ω
questions.solution: Bode: magnitude slope = -20 dB/decade per pole, +20 dB/decade per zero. Asymptotic plots.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Non-inverting: Rf=100kΩ, R₁=22kΩ, Vin=2V. Vout:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: BPSK modulation:
options: Phase of carrier shifts by 180° for bit change
questions.solution: BPSK: 0° for '0', 180° for '1'. Distance between symbols = 2√E_b. BER = 0.5·erfc(√(E_b/N₀)).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Parity bit for error detection can detect:
questions.solution: Parity: odd or even. Detects odd number of bit errors. Cannot correct errors.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The dominant mode in rectangular waveguide is:
questions.solution: TE₁₀: m=1, n=0. Cutoff: f_c = c/(2a). Waveguide dimensions: a > b, a = 2b typically.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Memory types — ROM:
options: Mask ROM (factory program), PROM (one-time), EPROM (UV erase), EEPROM (electrical erase), Flash
questions.solution: Flash: NAND (high density, USB drives) vs NOR (fast read, code storage).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Antenna directivity D vs gain G: G = e*D where e is:
questions.solution: Gain includes efficiency losses. Directivity is ideal. e = G/D <= 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Nyquist plot of G(s)=1/(s+1):
options: Semicircle from (1,0) to origin in fourth quadrant
questions.solution: G(jω)=1/(jω+1). At ω=0: 1. As ω→∞: 0. Phase from 0° to -90°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Routh-Hurwitz criterion:
options: Determines stability without solving characteristic equation
questions.solution: Routh array: if all elements in first column have same sign → stable. Number of sign changes = RHP poles.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: System type is determined by:
options: Number of integrators (poles at s=0) in the open-loop TF
questions.solution: Type 0: finite steady-state error to step. Type 1: zero step error, finite ramp error. Type 2: zero ramp error.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM carrier of amplitude 50 V is modulated by a 50 V message. The modulation index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RC low-pass: R=2.2kΩ, C=1μF. -3dB frequency:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PID tuning: Ziegler-Nichols ultimate gain method uses:
options: Ku (ultimate gain) and Tu (ultimate period) from sustained oscillations
questions.solution: Z-N: increase K until sustained oscillation. Ku = gain, Tu = period. Kp=0.6Ku, Ti=Tu/2, Td=Tu/8.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Matched filter maximizes:
questions.solution: Matched filter: h(t)=s(T-t). Maximizes S/N at t=T. Equivalent to correlator.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PCM SNR for 12-bit quantization:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An 16-bit PCM system has a signal-to-quantization-noise ratio of about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Eb/N0 (energy per bit / noise density):
options: Normalized SNR. Used for BER comparison. Eb/N0 = S/N × (B/R_b)
questions.solution: Eb/N0: modulation efficiency. BPSK: 9.6 dB for BER=10⁻⁵. QPSK: same Eb/N0 as BPSK.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An 12-bit PCM system has a signal-to-quantization-noise ratio of about:

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: OFDM cyclic prefix: main purpose:
questions.solution: CP: copies tail of OFDM symbol to front. Turns linear convolution to circular. Eliminates ISI from multipath.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM signal carries a message of highest frequency 3 kHz. Its transmission bandwidth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FM is generally preferred over AM for high-fidelity audio because it offers:
questions.solution: FM's constant amplitude makes it far more robust against amplitude noise than AM.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SSB modulation bandwidth is:
questions.solution: SSB: only one sideband transmitted. BW = B (same as message). AM: BW = 2B.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A link transmits 10 W with a net antenna gain of 100 and a path loss factor of 1000. The received power is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An LC tank has L = 10 µH and C = 10 pF. Its resonant frequency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a random process X(t)=A·cos(ω₀t+θ) where θ~U[0,2π], autocorrelation R(τ):

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM carrier of amplitude 100 V is modulated by a 30 V message. The modulation index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pulse modulation — PPM:
options: Pulse position varies with amplitude. Constant width, variable position
questions.solution: PPM: used in optical communications. Better noise immunity than PAM.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Digital modulation — ASK:
options: Amplitude shift keying. On-off keying (OOK). Simple but noise-sensitive
questions.solution: ASK: carrier on = binary 1, off = binary 0. Used in optical fiber.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Colpitts oscillator:
options: LC oscillator. Capacitive divider (C1, C2). f = 1/(2π√(L·C1C2/(C1+C2)))
questions.solution: Colpitts: tapped capacitor. Hartley: tapped inductor. Crystal: most stable.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Duplexing — TDD:
options: Time Division Duplex. Same frequency, alternate Tx/Rx. Unpaired spectrum
questions.solution: TDD: flexible (asymmetric). No guard band. 3G/4G TDD bands (B40, B41). 5G uses TDD heavily.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Class C amplifier achieves the highest efficiency but:
options: Conducts for less than half a cycle, producing heavy distortion
Conducts for the full cycle linearly
questions.solution: Class C conducts <180°, so it is efficient but non-linear — used with tuned RF loads.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RC phase shift oscillator:
options: 3 RC sections, 180° phase shift each. f = 1/(2π√6·RC)
questions.solution: Phase shift: 180° in RC network + 180° in CE amplifier. Frequency determined by RC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The gain-bandwidth product:
options: A constant for an op-amp (GBP = gain × bandwidth)
questions.solution: GBP = A × BW = constant. Op-amp with GBP = 1 MHz: gain=10 → BW=100 kHz.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about an inverting op-amp amplifier is correct?
options: It cannot have gain greater than 1
Its output is 180° out of phase with the input
questions.solution: The inverting amplifier inverts the signal (gain = −Rf/Rin).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The magnitude response shown is flat then rolls off at −20 dB/decade past f_c ≈ 320 Hz. This is the response of a:
questions.solution: Constant gain at low frequencies with a single −20 dB/decade roll-off above f_c ≈ 320 Hz is a first-order low-pass response.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy bandgap of Si at 300K is:
questions.solution: Si: 1.12 eV (indirect). Ge: 0.67 eV. GaAs: 1.43 eV (direct). Wider gap = higher temp capability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting amp: Rf=220.0kΩ, Rin=1.0kΩ. Gain:

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: ARM Cortex-M pipeline: number of stages in Cortex-M3:
questions.solution: Cortex-M3: 3-stage pipeline (fetch, decode, execute). Cortex-M7: 6-stage. Simpler pipeline = lower power.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sensitivity of closed-loop:
options: S = 1/(1+G(s)H(s)). Less sensitive to plant variations than open-loop
questions.solution: Sensitivity: small at low f (|GH| large). Increases near crossover. Trade-off with stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Second-order system: ω_n=10 rad/s, ζ=0.3. Percentage overshoot ≈

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Circular convolution vs linear: circular wraps around, to get linear via:
options: Zero-pad both sequences to length L+M-1
questions.solution: Linear convolution of length-L and length-M needs L+M-1 points. Zero-padding before DFT multiplication avoids time-domain aliasing.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quine-McCluskey:
options: Tabular minimization. For many variables. Prime implicant chart
questions.solution: Q-M: systematic. Finds all prime implicants. Minimal sum from covering chart.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A decoder with n input lines activates:
questions.solution: An n-to-2ⁿ decoder asserts exactly one output line for each input combination.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Multiple access — TDMA:
options: Each user assigned time slot. Digital. GSM: 8 users per 200 kHz carrier
questions.solution: TDMA: framing. GSM: 8 time slots × 114 bits each = 577 μs burst. Adaptive equalization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: QAM (Quadrature Amplitude Modulation):
questions.solution: QAM: constellation points. Higher spectral efficiency. 64-QAM: 6 bits/symbol. Used in WiFi, DVB.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Observer-based control: separation principle states:
options: Controller and observer can be designed independently
questions.solution: Separation: total controller = state feedback gain + observer. Eigenvalues of combined system = union of individual sets.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lead compensator maximum phase occurs at:
options: Geometric mean of pole and zero frequencies: ω_m=√(z·p)
questions.solution: Lead: ω_m = √(z·p). Max phase φ_m = sin⁻¹((1-α)/(1+α)) where α=p/z.
